title: "Drawing" the Mona Lisa with GPT-5.6, Claude, Gemini, and Grok
excerpt: Four frontier models, a blank canvas, and colored pencils. We tracked every stroke, dollar, and output as they tried to draw the Mona Lisa.
content_html:
We built a drawing arena: hand a model a blank white canvas and a set of colored-pencil tools, then get out of the way. The model sets a color, tip width, and pressure, lays down batches of strokes, smudges to blend, erases, and calls view_canvas to see its own work and decide what to fix. It either reproduces a target image or draws from a text prompt.

We ran four vision models, GPT-5.6 Sol, Claude Fable 5, Grok 4.5, and Gemini 3.6 Flash, across two targets (the Mona Lisa and Van Gogh's Starry Night, both scored objectively) and five open-ended prompts, for 28 drawings total. We'll cover tool use, cost, output, and whether the models actually improved their work, with our opinion at the end.

Why we run these

A quick note on why we're doing this, because our last one of these (models making music videos) kicked off a good discussion where some folks read it as us promoting the creativity or artistic ability of models. These are not objective tests of model capability. They are deliberately open-ended, fuzzy tasks. Here is why we personally keep running them:

  • They are fun, and genuinely informative. Watching a model tackle a loose, open-ended task is a more interesting visual indicator of capability than yet another benchmark number.
  • They expose the real frontier-vs-open gap. Cheaper open-weight models can absolutely replace the frontier for a lot of execution work, and we will keep reporting on that. But there is also a lot of benchmaxxing out there, and tasks like this cut through it. They genuinely separate frontier capability from the rest. Grok 4.5, as you will see, was rough at this "basic" drawing task, and the open-weight models we tried were not even usable, several just returned a blank canvas. (That may change with Kimi K3 which we will report on once it is fully open-sourced)
  • They show what long-running tasks actually cost. Claude Fable 5 almost always took much longer than the others, for far more money, and here produced worse output. Fable has beaten GPT-5.6 for us on plenty of tasks, including the music-video challenge, but on this one it was worse for a lot more overhead. Depending on your use case, that trade-off matters.
  • We're loving the discussions. The discussions have genuinely been interesting, and if you have suggestions for the setup or new tasks, we will take them into account. These have been a blast to run, and the outputs are super exciting to see.

The tools

Every model worked with the exact same colored-pencil toolset:

  • plan: a no-op scratchpad for thinking and planning between steps.
  • view_target: look at the target image again.
  • view_canvas: render the current page and see it. This is also when we score the drawing against the target.
  • set_color / set_brush / set_pressure: the current pencil color, tip width, and pressure (0 to 1 opacity, low pressure for softer tone).
  • draw: lay down a batch of marks (strokes, lines, shape outlines, dots) in one call. There are no solid fills, tone and color are built by layering, like a real pencil.
  • smudge: blend/soften a rectangular region, like a blending stump.
  • erase / clear_canvas: lift a region back to white / reset the whole page.

The whole harness is open source at github.com/hershalb/canvas-arena. Point it at any target image or a text prompt and run it yourself.

The two target reproductions

The models could see the target the whole time and were scored on structural similarity (SSIM) to it. We show the SSIM scores below.

The headline numbers

Averages and totals across all seven drawings per model.

Model

Claude Fable 575412.5 min14.6M$160.5815.6207

Gemini 3.6 Flash7736.9 min27.7M$12.8722.6190

Tool calling

The four models used the exact same toolset in completely different ways.

GPT-5.6 SolClaude Fable 5
Grok 4.5Gemini 3.6 Flash

GPT-5.6 Sol never called set_color, set_brush, or set_pressure once, it set those inline on each draw call instead, so its calls are almost all draw, smudge, and view_canvas. Grok 4.5 did the opposite: 65% of its 1,349 tool calls were set_color / set_brush / set_pressure, which is why it averaged 99 steps per drawing. Claude Fable 5 leaned on smudge (123 calls) and reviewed constantly. Gemini 3.6 Flash was the most obsessive reviewer of all, nearly a third of its calls were view_canvas (about 23 self-reviews per drawing), and like Sol it never touched the set_* tools.

Cost and tokens

Claude Fable 5 cost an estimated $160 for its seven drawings, roughly 20x GPT-5.6 Sol ($7.74), Grok 4.5 ($9.21), or Gemini 3.6 Flash ($12.87). This shouldn't really be a surprise by now. Grok used by far the most tokens (34M) but stayed cheap because ~98% were cached reads at a fraction of the input rate, and Grok's rates are the lowest of the four. Gemini also leaned heavily on cached reads, so even at 27.7M tokens it stayed modest.

Did the models actually improve?

Every view_canvas re-scored the canvas against the target, so we can watch progress over a run. Two patterns held across both targets:

First, they plateau early. Claude Fable 5 reviewed its Mona Lisa 27 times, but its similarity was essentially flat after about the fifth review. Second, in all eight target runs, the final drawing scored below the best the model reached mid-run. GPT-5.6 Sol's Mona Lisa peaked at 0.352 SSIM and ended at 0.325; its Starry Night peaked at 0.179 and ended at 0.130. Gemini 3.6 Flash reviewed the most (about 23 times per drawing) and reached the highest peak of the whole batch, 0.449 on the Mona Lisa, then slid all the way back to 0.337 by the end. More reviewing did not translate into a better finish. The models kept editing past their own best performance.

Objective similarity (target runs)

SSIM is 0 to 1, higher is closer to the target. RMSE is 0 to 255, lower is closer to the target.

Model
GPT-5.6 SolMona Lisa448m41s70.3250.35254.2
Claude Fable 5Mona Lisa8618m58s270.2860.28956.9
Grok 4.5Mona Lisa1064m30s50.1510.15295.6
Gemini 3.6 FlashMona Lisa605m31s220.3370.44951.1
GPT-5.6 SolStarry Night235m47s40.1300.17937.8
Claude Fable 5Starry Night4311m30s120.1530.17640.9
Grok 4.5Starry Night585m51s30.0390.039104.9
Gemini 3.6 FlashStarry Night746m36s240.1720.19042.8

On raw SSIM, Gemini 3.6 Flash scored highest on both targets, on final score and on peak, though it also swung the most between its best frame and its final one. Gemini 3.6 Flash looked decent, but definitely does not look the closest to the target output. Very interesting that the raw score turned out to be higher though. As always, SSIM measures structural closeness, not how good the drawing looks to a person (see our take below).

Method notes

  • Four models, two scored targets plus five prompts each, 28 drawings total. All runs used the same colored-pencil toolset. Reasoning was set to high for every model.
  • SSIM/RMSE are computed by resizing both images to 256x256 and comparing. They measure structural/pixel closeness, not artistic quality, and only apply to the target runs (prompts have no reference to score against).
  • Wall-clock time includes the model's own thinking and tool round-trips.

Our take

GPT-5.6 Sol was the runaway leader. Its Starry Night and its rose were our favorites of the whole batch, which is wild considering it drew them stroke by stroke on a blank canvas. It also beat the other two on detail in almost every drawing, the one exception being the elderly fisherman.

Grok 4.5 was basically garbage here. It rarely produced anything usable, and despite its frantic tool use I would not trust it to reliably understand or judge visual output yet.

Gemini 3.6 Flash was definitely better than Grok at a slight cost increase, but comparing the Flash model to frontier versions seems a little unfair. I'm definitely excited to see how Gemini 4 will perform. We also already knew this, but the token output speed is just so impressive.

Claude Fable 5 landed second on quality but at the far end on cost and time. For this task it was the least appealing trade-off of the three: much slower and far more expensive (about 20x the others) for output that did not keep up with Sol.

sentences.en: plan: a no-op scratchpad for thinking and planning between steps.
sentences.zh: 计划：用于在步骤之间进行思考和计划的无操作便签本。

blocks.html_en: • view_target: look at the target image again.

sentences.en: view_target: look at the target image again.
sentences.zh: view_target：再次查看目标图像。

blocks.html_en: • view_canvas: render the current page and see it. This is also when we score the drawing against the target.

sentences.en: view_canvas: render the current page and see it.
sentences.zh: view_canvas：渲染当前页面并查看它。

sentences.en: This is also when we score the drawing against the target.
sentences.zh: 这也是我们针对目标得分的时候。

blocks.html_en: • set_color / set_brush / set_pressure: the current pencil color, tip width, and pressure (0 to 1 opacity, low pressure for softer tone).

sentences.en: set_color / set_brush / set_pressure: the current pencil color, tip width, and pressure (0 to 1 opacity, low pressure for softer tone).
sentences.zh: set_color / set_brush / set_Pressure：当前铅笔颜色、笔尖宽度和压力（0 到 1 不透明度，低压使色调更柔和）。

blocks.html_en: • draw: lay down a batch of marks (strokes, lines, shape outlines, dots) in one call. There are no solid fills, tone and color are built by layering, like a real pencil.

sentences.en: draw: lay down a batch of marks (strokes, lines, shape outlines, dots) in one call.
sentences.zh: 绘制：在一次调用中放置一批标记（笔画、线条、形状轮廓、点）。

sentences.en: There are no solid fills, tone and color are built by layering, like a real pencil.
sentences.zh: 没有实心填充，色调和颜色是通过分层构建的，就像真正的铅笔一样。

blocks.html_en: • smudge: blend/soften a rectangular region, like a blending stump.

sentences.en: smudge: blend/soften a rectangular region, like a blending stump.
sentences.zh: 涂抹：混合/软化矩形区域，如混合树桩。

blocks.html_en: • erase / clear_canvas: lift a region back to white / reset the whole page.

sentences.en: erase / clear_canvas: lift a region back to white / reset the whole page.
sentences.zh: 擦除/清除画布：将某个区域恢复为白色/重置整个页面。

blocks.html_en: The whole harness is open source at github.com/hershalb/canvas-arena. Point it at any target image or a text prompt and run it yourself.

sentences.en: The whole harness is open source at github.com/hershalb/canvas-arena.
sentences.zh: 整个工具在 github.com/hershalb/canvas-arena 上开源。

sentences.en: Point it at any target image or a text prompt and run it yourself.
sentences.zh: 将其指向任何目标图像或文本提示并自行运行。

sentences.en: The two target reproductions
sentences.zh: 两个目标复制品

blocks.html_en: GPT-5.6 Sol never called set_color, set_brush, or set_pressure once, it set those inline on each draw call instead, so its calls are almost all draw, smudge, and view_canvas. Grok 4.5 did the opposite: 65% of its 1,349 tool calls were set_color / set_brush / set_pressure, which is why it averaged 99 steps per drawing. Claude Fable 5 leaned on smudge (123 calls) and reviewed constantly. Gemini 3.6 Flash was the most obsessive reviewer of all, nearly a third of its calls were view_canvas (about 23 self-reviews per drawing), and like Sol it never touched the set_* tools.

sentences.en: GPT-5.6 Sol never called set_color, set_brush, or set_pressure once, it set those inline on each draw call instead, so its calls are almost all draw, smudge, and view_canvas.
sentences.zh: GPT-5.6 Sol 从未调用过 set_color、set_brush 或 set_Pressure 一次，而是在每个绘制调用上内联设置这些内容，因此它的调用几乎都是绘制、涂抹和 view_canvas。

sentences.en: Grok 4.5 did the opposite: 65% of its 1,349 tool calls were set_color / set_brush / set_pressure, which is why it averaged 99 steps per drawing.
sentences.zh: Grok 4.5 则相反：其 1,349 个工具调用中有 65% 是 set_color / set_brush / set_Pressure，这就是为什么它平均每张图需要 99 个步骤。

sentences.en: Claude Fable 5 leaned on smudge (123 calls) and reviewed constantly.
sentences.zh: 《克劳德寓言 5》依靠污迹（123 个调用）并不断审查。

sentences.en: Gemini 3.6 Flash was the most obsessive reviewer of all, nearly a third of its calls were view_canvas (about 23 self-reviews per drawing), and like Sol it never touched the set_* tools.
sentences.zh: Gemini 3.6 Flash 是所有审阅者中最痴迷的，它的近三分之一的调用是 view_canvas（每张图大约 23 次自我审阅），并且像 Sol 一样，它从未触及 set_* 工具。

sentences.en: Cost and tokens
sentences.zh: 成本和代币

blocks.html_en: Claude Fable 5 cost an estimated $160 for its seven drawings, roughly 20x GPT-5.6 Sol ($7.74), Grok 4.5 ($9.21), or Gemini 3.6 Flash ($12.87). This shouldn't really be a surprise by now. Grok used by far the most tokens (34M) but stayed cheap because ~98% were cached reads at a fraction of the input rate, and Grok's rates are the lowest of the four. Gemini also leaned heavily on cached reads, so even at 27.7M tokens it stayed modest.

sentences.en: Claude Fable 5 cost an estimated $160 for its seven drawings, roughly 20x GPT-5.6 Sol ($7.74), Grok 4.5 ($9.21), or Gemini 3.6 Flash ($12.87).
sentences.zh: 《克劳德寓言 5》的七张图画售价估计为 160 美元，大约是 GPT-5.6 Sol（7.74 美元）、Grok 4.5（9.21 美元）或 Gemini 3.6 Flash（12.87 美元）的 20 倍。

sentences.en: This shouldn't really be a surprise by now.
sentences.zh: 现在这应该不足为奇。

sentences.en: Grok used by far the most tokens (34M) but stayed cheap because ~98% were cached reads at a fraction of the input rate, and Grok's rates are the lowest of the four.
sentences.zh: Grok 迄今为止使用了最多的令牌 (34M)，但仍然很便宜，因为约 98% 的缓存读取仅占输入速率的一小部分，而 Grok 的速率是四个中最低的。

sentences.en: Gemini also leaned heavily on cached reads, so even at 27.7M tokens it stayed modest.
sentences.zh: Gemini 还严重依赖缓存读取，因此即使有 2770 万个令牌，它仍然保持适度。

sentences.en: Did the models actually improve?
sentences.zh: 模型真的有所改进吗？

blocks.html_en: Every view_canvas re-scored the canvas against the target, so we can watch progress over a run. Two patterns held across both targets:

sentences.en: Every view_canvas re-scored the canvas against the target, so we can watch progress over a run.
sentences.zh: 每个 view_canvas 都会根据目标重新对画布进行评分，因此我们可以观察运行的进度。

sentences.en: Two patterns held across both targets:
sentences.zh: 两个目标都有两种模式：

blocks.html_en: First, they plateau early. Claude Fable 5 reviewed its Mona Lisa 27 times, but its similarity was essentially flat after about the fifth review. Second, in all eight target runs, the final drawing scored below the best the model reached mid-run. GPT-5.6 Sol's Mona Lisa peaked at 0.352 SSIM and ended at 0.325; its Starry Night peaked at 0.179 and ended at 0.130. Gemini 3.6 Flash reviewed the most (about 23 times per drawing) and reached the highest peak of the whole batch, 0.449 on the Mona Lisa, then slid all the way back to 0.337 by the end. More reviewing did not translate into a better finish. The models kept editing past their own best performance.

sentences.en: First, they plateau early.
sentences.zh: 首先，他们很早就达到了稳定状态。

sentences.en: Claude Fable 5 reviewed its Mona Lisa 27 times, but its similarity was essentially flat after about the fifth review.
sentences.zh: 《克劳德寓言5》对《蒙娜丽莎》进行了27次评论，但在大约第五次评论后，其相似度基本持平。

sentences.en: Second, in all eight target runs, the final drawing scored below the best the model reached mid-run.
sentences.zh: 其次，在所有八次目标运行中，最终绘图的得分低于模型在运行中达到的最佳值。

sentences.en: GPT-5.6 Sol's Mona Lisa peaked at 0.352 SSIM and ended at 0.325; its Starry Night peaked at 0.179 and ended at 0.130.
sentences.zh: GPT-5.6 Sol 的《蒙娜丽莎》峰值为 0.352 SSIM，结束为 0.325；其星夜峰值为0.179，结束于0.130。

sentences.en: Gemini 3.6 Flash reviewed the most (about 23 times per drawing) and reached the highest peak of the whole batch, 0.449 on the Mona Lisa, then slid all the way back to 0.337 by the end.
sentences.zh: Gemini 3.6 Flash 审核次数最多（每图约 23 次），在《蒙娜丽莎》上达到了全批次的最高峰，为 0.449，然后一路下滑至最后的 0.337。

sentences.en: More reviewing did not translate into a better finish.
sentences.zh: 更多的复习并不意味着更好的完成。

sentences.en: The models kept editing past their own best performance.
sentences.zh: 模特们不断地超越自己的最佳表现。

blocks.html_en: On raw SSIM, Gemini 3.6 Flash scored highest on both targets, on final score and on peak, though it also swung the most between its best frame and its final one. Gemini 3.6 Flash looked decent, but definitely does not look the closest to the target output. Very interesting that the raw score turned out to be higher though. As always, SSIM measures structural closeness, not how good the drawing looks to a person (see our take below).

sentences.en: On raw SSIM, Gemini 3.6 Flash scored highest on both targets, on final score and on peak, though it also swung the most between its best frame and its final one.
sentences.zh: 在原始 SSIM 上，Gemini 3.6 Flash 在最终得分和峰值这两个目标上都得分最高，尽管它在最佳帧和最终帧之间的波动也最大。

sentences.en: Gemini 3.6 Flash looked decent, but definitely does not look the closest to the target output.
sentences.zh: Gemini 3.6 Flash 看起来还不错，但绝对不是最接近目标输出的。

sentences.en: Very interesting that the raw score turned out to be higher though.
sentences.zh: 非常有趣的是，原始分数结果更高。

sentences.en: As always, SSIM measures structural closeness, not how good the drawing looks to a person (see our take below).
sentences.zh: 与往常一样，SSIM 衡量的是结构的紧密程度，而不是绘图对人的外观有多好（请参阅下面的内容）。

sentences.en: Method notes
sentences.zh: 方法注释

blocks.html_en: • Four models, two scored targets plus five prompts each, 28 drawings total. All runs used the same colored-pencil toolset. Reasoning was set to high for every model.

sentences.en: Four models, two scored targets plus five prompts each, 28 drawings total.
sentences.zh: 四个模型，两个得分目标，每个目标加上五个提示，总共 28 张图画。

sentences.en: All runs used the same colored-pencil toolset.
sentences.zh: 所有运行都使用相同的彩色铅笔工具集。

sentences.en: Reasoning was set to high for every model.
sentences.zh: 每个模型的推理都设置为高。

blocks.html_en: • SSIM/RMSE are computed by resizing both images to 256x256 and comparing. They measure structural/pixel closeness, not artistic quality, and only apply to the target runs (prompts have no reference to score against).

sentences.en: SSIM/RMSE are computed by resizing both images to 256x256 and comparing.
sentences.zh: SSIM/RMSE 是通过将两个图像的大小调整为 256x256 并进行比较来计算的。

sentences.en: They measure structural/pixel closeness, not artistic quality, and only apply to the target runs (prompts have no reference to score against).
sentences.zh: 它们测量结构/像素的接近度，而不是艺术质量，并且仅适用于目标运行（提示没有参考得分）。

blocks.html_en: • Wall-clock time includes the model's own thinking and tool round-trips.

sentences.en: Wall-clock time includes the model's own thinking and tool round-trips.
sentences.zh: 挂钟时间包括模型自己的思考和工具往返。

sentences.en: Our take
sentences.zh: 我们的看法

blocks.html_en: GPT-5.6 Sol was the runaway leader. Its Starry Night and its rose were our favorites of the whole batch, which is wild considering it drew them stroke by stroke on a blank canvas. It also beat the other two on detail in almost every drawing, the one exception being the elderly fisherman.

sentences.en: GPT-5.6 Sol was the runaway leader.
sentences.zh: GPT-5.6 Sol 是失控的领导者。

sentences.en: Its Starry Night and its rose were our favorites of the whole batch, which is wild considering it drew them stroke by stroke on a blank canvas.
sentences.zh: 它的《星夜》和《玫瑰》是整批作品中我们最喜欢的，考虑到它是在空白画布上一笔一笔地画出来的，这真是太疯狂了。

sentences.en: It also beat the other two on detail in almost every drawing, the one exception being the elderly fisherman.
sentences.zh: 它几乎在每幅画的细节上都击败了其他两幅画，唯一的例外是年长的渔夫。

blocks.html_en: Grok 4.5 was basically garbage here. It rarely produced anything usable, and despite its frantic tool use I would not trust it to reliably understand or judge visual output yet.

sentences.en: Grok 4.5 was basically garbage here.
sentences.zh: Grok 4.5 在这里基本上是垃圾。

sentences.en: It rarely produced anything usable, and despite its frantic tool use I would not trust it to reliably understand or judge visual output yet.
sentences.zh: 它很少产生任何可用的东西，尽管它使用了疯狂的工具，但我还不相信它能够可靠地理解或判断视觉输出。

blocks.html_en: Gemini 3.6 Flash was definitely better than Grok at a slight cost increase, but comparing the Flash model to frontier versions seems a little unfair. I'm definitely excited to see how Gemini 4 will perform. We also already knew this, but the token output speed is just so impressive.

sentences.en: Gemini 3.6 Flash was definitely better than Grok at a slight cost increase, but comparing the Flash model to frontier versions seems a little unfair.
sentences.zh: Gemini 3.6 Flash 肯定比 Grok 更好，但成本略有增加，但将 Flash 模型与前沿版本进行比较似乎有点不公平。

sentences.en: I'm definitely excited to see how Gemini 4 will perform.
sentences.zh: 我非常期待 Gemini 4 的表现。

sentences.en: We also already knew this, but the token output speed is just so impressive.

content_html_zh:
我们建造了一个绘画场地：给模特一张空白的白色画布和一套彩色铅笔工具，然后让开。 该模型设置颜色、笔尖宽度和压力，放置批量笔画、混合污迹、擦除，并调用 view_canvas 来查看其自己的工作并决定修复哪些内容。 它要么再现目标图像，要么根据文本提示进行绘制。

我们在两个目标（蒙娜丽莎和梵高的星夜，均客观得分）和五个开放式提示中运行了四种视觉模型：GPT-5.6 Sol、Claude Fable 5、Grok 4.5 和 Gemini 3.6 Flash，总共 28 张图画。 我们将讨论工具的使用、成本、产出以及模型是否真正改进了他们的工作，并在最后提出我们的意见。

为什么我们运行这些

简单说明一下我们为什么要这样做，因为我们的最后一个（模特制作音乐视频）引发了一场很好的讨论，一些人在我们促进模特的创造力或艺术能力时阅读了它。 这些不是模型能力的客观测试。 它们是故意开放式的、模糊的任务。 这就是我们个人继续运行它们的原因：

  • 它们很有趣，而且内容丰富。 观察模型处理松散的、开放式的任务是比另一个基准数字更有趣的视觉能力指标。
  • 它们暴露了真正的前沿与开放的差距。 更便宜的开放重量模型绝对可以取代大量执行工作的前沿，我们将继续对此进行报道。 但也有很多板凳最大化，像这样的任务会贯穿其中。 他们真正将前沿能力与其他能力区分开来。 正如您将看到的，Grok 4.5 在这个“基本”绘图任务上很粗糙，我们尝试的开放重量模型甚至无法使用，有几个只是返回了空白画布。 （Kimi K3 可能会改变这种情况，一旦它完全开源，我们将对其进行报告）
  • 它们显示了长时间运行的任务的实际成本。 《克劳德寓言5》几乎总是比其他作品花费更长的时间，花费更多的钱，而这里的产出却较差。 《神鬼寓言》在很多任务上都击败了 GPT-5.6，包括音乐视频挑战，但在这一项上，情况更糟，因为开销要大得多。 根据您的用例，这种权衡很重要。
  • 我们很喜欢讨论。 讨论确实很有趣，如果您对设置或新任务有建议，我们会考虑它们。 这些运行起来真是太棒了，而且输出也非常令人兴奋。

工具

每个模型都使用完全相同的彩色铅笔工具集：

  • 计划：用于在步骤之间进行思考和计划的无操作便签本。
  • view_target：再次查看目标图像。
  • view_canvas：渲染当前页面并查看它。 这也是我们针对目标得分的时候。
  • set_color / set_brush / set_Pressure：当前铅笔颜色、笔尖宽度和压力（0 到 1 不透明度，低压使色调更柔和）。
  • 绘制：在一次调用中放置一批标记（笔画、线条、形状轮廓、点）。 没有实心填充，色调和颜色是通过分层构建的，就像真正的铅笔一样。
  • 涂抹：混合/软化矩形区域，如混合树桩。
  • 擦除/清除画布：将某个区域恢复为白色/重置整个页面。

整个工具在 github.com/hershalb/canvas-arena 上开源。 将其指向任何目标图像或文本提示并自行运行。

两个目标复制品

这些模型可以始终看到目标，并根据与其的结构相似性 (SSIM) 进行评分。 我们在下面显示了 SSIM 分数。

工具调用

这四个模型以完全不同的方式使用完全相同的工具集。

GPT-5.6溶胶克劳德寓言5
Grok 4.5双子座3.6闪存

GPT-5.6 Sol 从未调用过 set_color、set_brush 或 set_Pressure 一次，而是在每个绘制调用上内联设置这些内容，因此它的调用几乎都是绘制、涂抹和 view_canvas。 Grok 4.5 则相反：其 1,349 个工具调用中有 65% 是 set_color / set_brush / set_Pressure，这就是为什么它平均每张图需要 99 个步骤。 《克劳德寓言 5》依靠污迹（123 个调用）并不断审查。 Gemini 3.6 Flash 是所有审阅者中最痴迷的，它的近三分之一的调用是 view_canvas（每张图大约 23 次自我审阅），并且像 Sol 一样，它从未触及 set_* 工具。

成本和代币

《克劳德寓言 5》的七张图画售价估计为 160 美元，大约是 GPT-5.6 Sol（7.74 美元）、Grok 4.5（9.21 美元）或 Gemini 3.6 Flash（12.87 美元）的 20 倍。 现在这应该不足为奇。 Grok 迄今为止使用了最多的令牌 (34M)，但仍然很便宜，因为约 98% 的缓存读取仅占输入速率的一小部分，而 Grok 的速率是四个中最低的。 Gemini 还严重依赖缓存读取，因此即使有 2770 万个令牌，它仍然保持适度。

模型真的有所改进吗？

每个 view_canvas 都会根据目标重新对画布进行评分，因此我们可以观察运行的进度。 两个目标都有两种模式：

首先，他们很早就达到了稳定状态。 《克劳德寓言5》对《蒙娜丽莎》进行了27次评论，但在大约第五次评论后，其相似度基本持平。 其次，在所有八次目标运行中，最终绘图的得分低于模型在运行中达到的最佳值。 GPT-5.6 Sol 的《蒙娜丽莎》峰值为 0.352 SSIM，结束为 0.325；其星夜峰值为0.179，结束于0.130。 Gemini 3.6 Flash 审核次数最多（每图约 23 次），在《蒙娜丽莎》上达到了全批次的最高峰，为 0.449，然后一路下滑至最后的 0.337。 更多的复习并不意味着更好的完成。 模特们不断地超越自己的最佳表现。

在原始 SSIM 上，Gemini 3.6 Flash 在最终得分和峰值这两个目标上都得分最高，尽管它在最佳帧和最终帧之间的波动也最大。 Gemini 3.6 Flash 看起来还不错，但绝对不是最接近目标输出的。 非常有趣的是，原始分数结果更高。 与往常一样，SSIM 衡量的是结构的紧密程度，而不是绘图对人的外观有多好（请参阅下面的内容）。

方法注释

  • 四个模型，两个得分目标，每个目标加上五个提示，总共 28 张图画。 所有运行都使用相同的彩色铅笔工具集。 每个模型的推理都设置为高。
  • SSIM/RMSE 是通过将两个图像的大小调整为 256x256 并进行比较来计算的。 它们测量结构/像素的接近度，而不是艺术质量，并且仅适用于目标运行（提示没有参考得分）。
  • 挂钟时间包括模型自己的思考和工具往返。

我们的看法

GPT-5.6 Sol 是失控的领导者。 它的《星夜》和《玫瑰》是整批作品中我们最喜欢的，考虑到它是在空白画布上一笔一笔地画出来的，这真是太疯狂了。 它几乎在每幅画的细节上都击败了其他两幅画，唯一的例外是年长的渔夫。

Grok 4.5 在这里基本上是垃圾。 它很少产生任何可用的东西，尽管它使用了疯狂的工具，但我还不相信它能够可靠地理解或判断视觉输出。

Gemini 3.6 Flash 肯定比 Grok 更好，但成本略有增加，但将 Flash 模型与前沿版本进行比较似乎有点不公平。 我非常期待 Gemini 4 的表现。 我们也已经知道这一点，但代币输出速度真是令人印象深刻。

《克劳德寓言 5》在质量上排名第二，但在成本和时间上却垫底。 对于这项任务来说，这是三者中最没有吸引力的权衡：速度慢得多，成本也高得多（大约是其他任务的 20 倍），因为输出无法跟上 Sol。
